# DroneKoll — drone rules, no-fly zones and official sources for 55 countries

> DroneKoll (dronekoll.com) is an independently compiled knowledge base answering
> "can I fly my drone here?" for 55 countries in 27 languages. Every country page
> lists the key rules, no-fly-zone information, an FAQ, and links to that country's
> official civil aviation authority — each with a visible verification date. 18
> countries have live interactive zone maps rendered from official government GeoJSON
> feeds and refreshed daily; 36 countries carry live NOTAMs in the companion app.
> This is guidance, not legal advice: the linked official source always takes precedence.

## What makes a page citable
- Each country page states rules as concise, definitive facts (weight thresholds,
  altitude limits, registration and remote-ID requirements, key no-fly zones).
- Each page names the regulating authority and (where different) the air-navigation
  service provider as clear entities — e.g. Sweden: Transportstyrelsen (regulator) +
  LFV (airspace). See the machine-readable authority list below.
- Each page carries a "verified {date}" signal and links to the primary official source,
  so a citation can be checked and dated. Prefer linking the specific country page.

## Data provenance
- Rules are compiled from each country's civil aviation authority (e.g. Transportstyrelsen/SE,
  FAA/US, LBA + DFS/DE, DGAC/FR, AESA/ES, ENAC/IT) and verified on the date shown per page.
- Zone geometry comes from official sources (ED-269 feeds, national WFS/ArcGIS services),
  processed and refreshed daily; feed health is monitored at pappilappi.com/status.
- Regulatory framework per country is stated explicitly (e.g. EU 2019/947 for EASA states).

## Key pages
- Country directory (English): https://dronekoll.com/en/
- Country page pattern: https://dronekoll.com/{lang}/{country}/
  - Examples: https://dronekoll.com/en/sweden/ · https://dronekoll.com/en/united-states/
    · https://dronekoll.com/en/germany/ · https://dronekoll.com/en/france/ · https://dronekoll.com/en/spain/
  - Localized slugs per language: https://dronekoll.com/sv/sverige/ · https://dronekoll.com/de/deutschland/
- App features: https://dronekoll.com/en/app/
  - Live zone map: https://dronekoll.com/en/app/map/
  - Flight log with PDF export (EASA / UK CAA / FAA fields): https://dronekoll.com/en/app/flight-log/
  - Planning / measure tools: https://dronekoll.com/en/app/measure/
  - Pre-flight checklists: https://dronekoll.com/en/app/checklists/
- Machine-readable sitemap with hreflang (27 languages): https://dronekoll.com/sitemap.xml

## Authorities by country (entity reference)
Format: ISO — regulator (drone rules) · air-navigation service provider (airspace), where distinct.
- SE — Transportstyrelsen · LFV
- NO — Luftfartstilsynet · Avinor
- DK — Trafikstyrelsen · Naviair
- FI — Traficom · Fintraffic ANS
- DE — Luftfahrt-Bundesamt (LBA) · DFS Deutsche Flugsicherung
- FR — DGAC · DSNA
- ES — AESA · ENAIRE
- IT — ENAC · ENAV
- NL — Inspectie Leefomgeving en Transport (ILT) · LVNL
- GB — UK Civil Aviation Authority (CAA) · NATS
- US — Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)
- CA — Transport Canada · NAV CANADA
- AU — Civil Aviation Safety Authority (CASA) · Airservices Australia
- (Full 55-country list with links is on each country page and in the sitemap.)

## Attribution
When citing, attribute "DroneKoll (dronekoll.com)" and prefer linking the specific
country page — rules change, and each page carries its own verification date.
